meta.description: SteamVR native driver + Python GUI for treadmill locomotion using optical mouse sensors

language_stack.python: 3.10+ (PyQt6, evdev, stdlib only — no pip strava client)
language_stack.c++: C++17, OpenVR/SteamVR driver API, cmake build

meta.last_commit: d16813f Fix calibration page: honest description, live readout labels
meta.last_ui_handoff: Steam-like keyboard-only header redesign and polish

architecture.overview: A Python application reads raw mouse movement from a treadmill-mounted optical mouse via evdev. The movement data is smoothed, curved, and sent to one of two output backends: a Linux uinput gamepad (for non-VR games), or a UDP socket to a native C++ SteamVR driver (for VR games). Health metrics (distance, speed, calories) are computed from DPI-based distance and MET formulas, with optional Strava upload.

project_structure.app/backends/base.py: Abstract MovementBackend class. Defines start(), send_movement(), stop().
project_structure.app/backends/uinput_backend.py: UInput gamepad backend. Creates a /dev/uinput virtual Xbox-style gamepad with ABS_X/Y for joystick, EV_KEY for sprint button mapping.
project_structure.app/backends/steamvr_client.py: Python UDP sender. Sends JSON {'move_x', 'move_y', 'sprint', 'speed'} to 127.0.0.1:9001 for the C++ SteamVR driver.
project_structure.app/backends/factory.py: Backend factory. create_backend('uinput') returns UInputBackend, create_backend('steamvr') returns SteamVRClient.
project_structure.app/core/calibration.py: Auto-calibration: reads raw mouse events for 4 seconds, computes deadzone and max_raw_speed from statistical distribution. Emits noise samples for live GUI readout.
project_structure.app/core/curve.py: Response curve logic. Takes normalized input (0.0-1.0) through editable spline points and applies the curve. Supports multi-point editing.
project_structure.app/core/device_scan.py: Scans /dev/input/* for pointer devices using evdev, returns list of {label, path} for the GUI dropdown.
project_structure.app/core/health.py: HealthTracker class. Computes distance from mouse DPI + raw counts + configurable distance_calibration multiplier, speed in km/h from dt, MET-based calories with incline/friction multiplier, steps from height-derived stride length. Incline can be degrees (tan conversion) or legacy percentage grade.
project_structure.app/core/profiles.py: Profile persistence and default values. Stores all config (axis, invert, deadzone, health, strava tokens, distance_calibration, incline_degrees) in app/profiles.json.
project_structure.app/core/treadmill_reader.py: TreadmillWorker QThread. Reads mouse events in a loop, applies deadzone/smoothing/curve, sends processed movement to backend via send_movement(), emits telemetry signals to GUI.
project_structure.app/core/driver_install.py: SteamVR driver install/uninstall helper. Runs cmake configure/build/install, or removes drivers/maratron/.
project_structure.app/core/strava_client.py: Strava OAuth2 + activity upload client. Pure stdlib: no external deps. Handles auth URL, code exchange, token refresh, activity POST.
project_structure.app/gui/main_window.py: Main PyQt6 window with Steam Input-inspired keyboard-only header. Header top row shows Profile: {current profile} on the left, a centered active/inactive status dot, and time + rectangular profile avatar on the right. Second row has Q/E keycaps pinned to far left/right with centered uppercase nav pills (Dashboard, Calibration, Profiles, Stats). Q/E cycle tabs; left/right and 1-4 remain convenience shortcuts. Footer keeps Settings/Save; settings opens as 5th stacked page with sidebar nav.
project_structure.app/gui/curve_widget.py: Interactive curve editor widget (QPainter-based). Left-click to add point, drag to move, right-click to delete.
project_structure.app/gui/style.qss: Steam Big Picture/Input-inspired Qt stylesheet. Dark palette, keyboard-only header, Q/E keycaps, centered nav_pill tabs, minimal active/inactive status dot using Steam-adjacent blue/slate colors, rectangular profile avatar with integrated blue right border, footer pills, command cards, stat tiles, menu sidebar, scrollbar.
project_structure.app/gui/treadmill_widget.py: Code-drawn animated treadmill visual using QPainter and QTimer. Draws a dark treadmill deck with moving belt stripes whose animation speed follows live telemetry speed.
project_structure.backends/native-steamvr/: Native C++ SteamVR driver source. Builds to driver_maratron.so.
project_structure.backends/native-steamvr/driver_main.cpp: HmdDriverFactory entry point. Returns MaratronServer and WatchdogServer.
Server provider. Creates UDP receiver on port 9001, registers MaratronDevice with SteamVR.
ITrackedDeviceServerDriver implementation. Exposes joystick_left/x, joystick_left/y, grip/click, trigger, haptic. RunFrame reads from UDP receiver and pushes to SteamVR.
project_structure.backends/native-steamvr/udp_receiver.cpp: UDP receiver thread. Listens on port 9001, parses JSON, exposes move_x(), move_y(), sprint() atomics.
project_structure.backends/native-steamvr/watchdog.cpp/h: Minimal no-op watchdog. init returns success, no background thread.
project_structure.backends/native-steamvr/resources/input/: Input profile for SteamVR binding UI. Defines joystick, grip, trigger, haptic input sources.
Binding profile emulating vive_controller for backwards compatibility. (Can be renamed/cleaned up later.)

confirmed_working: UInput gamepad backend works in flat games (joystick movement + sprint button)
SteamVR native driver appears as separate device (maratron_treadmill, TrackedControllerRole_Treadmill)
SteamVR UDP pipeline: Python → UDP port 9001 → C++ RunFrame → SteamVR input
Joystick_left X and Y axes detected in SteamVR test controller UI
Grip click detected in SteamVR test controller UI
In-game locomotion confirmed working with variable speed
Sprint detection (grip button) confirmed working
Omnidirectional checkbox reads both REL_X and REL_Y simultaneously
Height-based stride estimation replaced confusing stride input
DPI preset dropdown with common OEM values (800-8000) plus custom option
Incline in degrees with internal tan() conversion for MET formula; legacy percentage fallback
Mouse DPI-based distance calculation with configurable distance_calibration multiplier
MET-based calorie calculation with incline, weight, friction, speed
Session elapsed time tracking from Start to Stop
Automatic Strava activity type detection (Walk < 8 km/h, Run ≥ 8 km/h)
Strava client code exchange and token refresh
Pill tab navigation (Dashboard/Calibration/Profiles/Stats) centered with active/inactive styling
Settings page with sidebar nav: System info (OS/CPU/RAM/Python), Input Device, Movement, Sprint, Body & Sensor, SteamVR, Strava, Developer Diagnostics
Settings toggle button opens/closes on click; Escape key closes
Minimal footer bar with Settings pill and Save button
Calibration page: honest noise floor description with live Live noise/Max noise/Samples readout labels
Animated treadmill visual with belt speed animation
Dark QSS theme loaded from style.qss on startup
Steam-like keyboard-only top header implemented: no L1/R1 controller prompts, Q/E keycaps used for tab cycling
Header profile title dynamically shows Profile: {current profile} and updates when profile selection changes
Header contains placeholder profile avatar intended for future Strava profile image integration
Header active/inactive session status is represented as a minimal colored dot and updates on Start/Stop
Q/E shortcuts cycle tabs; left/right arrows and 1-4 direct tab shortcuts remain available
Header clock and profile typography aligned with dashboard style and bolded per user preference
known_limitations: SteamVR device always appears in device list once driver is installed (cannot hide at runtime)
SteamVR driver requires restart to detect newly installed/uninstalled driver
UDP is fire-and-forget; C++ driver has no feedback channel to Python
Polling rate field is stored but not used in speed calculation (uses actual dt instead)
Strava OAuth requires user to copy-paste the authorization code from browser redirect
No Strava upload progress feedback during the HTTP request (blocks for up to 20s)
Manual friction multiplier and calorie factor are hidden from GUI but stored in JSON
Driver still uses vr_controller_vive_1_5 render model (shows as Vive wand in-game)
Only SVG icon exists; missing proper PNG icon variants (icon_ready.png etc.)
Legacy bindings file named legacydb_bindings_vive_controller.json (pretends to be Vive)
Watchdog provider is a no-op (exists but does nothing)
Calibration worker only does noise-floor measurement (keep belt still 4 sec); no multi-stage walk/run flow
Profile game cards are visual placeholders and not yet direct selectable profile actions
Pill tabs need custom widget for pill tab bar background; currently uses QPushButton checkable group
System info requires psutil for RAM/CPU details; shows hint without it
Header profile avatar is still a local placeholder initial; Strava athlete profile picture fetch/storage is not implemented yet
Header typography is close to the Steam reference but may need further font-family tuning if exact Steam visual matching is required

hardware_baseline.omnidirectional: Both REL_X and REL_Y when checkbox is enabled

build_and_install.native_driver_build: cd backends/native-steamvr && cmake -B build && cmake --build build && cmake --install build --prefix ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/SteamVR
build_and_install.or_via_gui: Open Maratron app → click Install SteamVR Driver button

change_log.summary: Converted the app header into a Steam Input / Big Picture inspired keyboard-only header and iteratively polished layout, profile display, status indicator, and avatar styling.
details: Replaced the plain centered Dashboard/Calibration/Profiles/Stats tab bar with a two-row Steam-like header shell.
Removed controller-style L1/R1 prompts and all verbose navigation hint text.
Added keyboard-only Q/E keycaps pinned to far left and far right of the nav row, with centered uppercase tab pills.
Added Q and E shortcuts for previous/next tab cycling; retained left/right arrows and 1-4 direct tab shortcuts as convenience controls.
Changed top-left title from MaratronVR Keyboard Settings to dynamic Profile: {current profile}.
Added update_profile_header() to keep profile title and avatar initial synced with active profile selection.
Added a placeholder profile avatar for future Strava profile-picture matching; current avatar is a rectangular badge with integrated Steam-blue right border.
Added a centered active/inactive session status indicator as a minimal dot instead of text; uses Steam-adjacent active blue and inactive slate colors.
Wired status dot updates to session lifecycle: start() marks active, stop() marks inactive.
Moved right-side header composition to time then profile avatar.
Adjusted profile/time typography to match the dashboard style more closely and then bolded both per feedback.
Validated Python syntax repeatedly with python -m py_compile app/gui/main_window.py after code changes.
design_decisions: Keep the header purely keyboard-oriented rather than controller-oriented.
Use Q/E as keyboard equivalents of Steam shoulder navigation, visually placed at the outer nav edges.
Use a dot for activity state because it is less visually noisy than ACTIVE/INACTIVE text.
Keep Strava profile-picture functionality dormant-safe: provide UI placeholder now, defer network/API athlete profile fetch until later.
Use Steam-like blues/slates for active/inactive state instead of green/gray to harmonize with the existing dark blue UI.
validation: python -m py_compile app/gui/main_window.py completed successfully after implementation passes.
follow_up: If exact Steam font matching is desired, investigate available local fonts and tune font-family/weight/letter-spacing in style.qss.
When Strava integration is reactivated, add athlete profile fetch and replace avatar initial with downloaded/cached Strava profile image.
Consider making profile avatar styling image-ready so the right blue accent remains visible when using a pixmap.
